channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Karen Read judge sued over 'buffer zone' keeping protesters away from courthouse
item.description: An anti-protester "buffer zone" around the Norfolk Superior Court where Karen Read is on trial in the death of John O'Keefe is challenged in a federal lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU Parliament has transparency problems. Marine Le Pen's case is a window into what's wrong
item.description: The conviction of one of the most powerful figures of the European far right for embezzling EU Parliament funds has sent shockwaves around the continent and beyond. But Marine Le Pen's case is just one example of transparency problems that have plagued the legislature.
item.dc:creator: ELLA JOYNER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Violent storms cut through the South and Midwest, spawning tornadoes and killing 1 person
item.description: Violent storms have cut through a wide swath of the South and Midwest, spawning tornadoes and killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees and ripping roofs off homes.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bodega cats make New Yorkers' hearts purr, even if they violate state regulations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City’s “bodega cats” are beloved fixtures in the Big Apple — but they’re on the wrong side of the law.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate rebukes Trump’s tariffs as some Republicans vote to halt taxes on Canadian imports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a resolution Wednesday night that would thwart President Donald Trump’s ability to impose tariffs on Canada, delivering a rare rebuke to the president just hours after he unveiled sweeping plans to clamp down on international trade.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's major tariff announcement
item.description: “Our country and its taxpayers have been ripped off for more than 50 years, but it’s not going to happen anymore,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU sues Trump administration over NIH grant cancellations
item.description: The lawsuit, filed in Massachusetts district court, said there was no “scientifically-valid explanation or cause” for NIH to cancel the research projects.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nevada girl, 7, killed in Lake Tahoe ski resort boulder accident: police
item.description: A 7-year-old girl died after being struck by a falling boulder at a ski resort near Lake Tahoe in Nevada, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Naval Academy removes hundreds of library books in DEI purge
item.description: Officials for the Annapolis, Md., school were instructed to review the library late last week, though it is unclear if the directive came straight from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beloved Anchorage pet reindeer euthanized after someone tampered with pen, possibly poisoned him
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An iconic pet reindeer beloved by many in Alaska's largest city has been euthanized, just weeks after someone tampered with his cage and possibly poisoned him, his caretaker said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Monthly mortgage payments soar to record high
item.description: A typical monthly housing payment hit a record high during the last four weeks ending March 23, according to a study by Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 key takeaways as Trump’s sweeping HHS layoffs begin
item.description: Thousands of employees across the vast Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) began receiving layoff notices as early as 5 a.m. on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A judge in Boston is holding a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent in contempt after he detained a suspect while the man was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Volcano erupts in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town, spa evacuated
item.description: A volcano in southwestern Iceland that has erupted repeatedly for more than a year again belched lava and smoke into the air on Tuesday, just hours after authorities evacuated the few remaining residents of a nearby fishing village.
item.dc:creator: MARCO Di MARCO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McMahon meeting with House Democrats over Education Department layoffs
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon is having a meeting with House Democrats on Wednesday over their concerns surrounding the layoffs at the Department of Education, which she and President Trump have been vocal about trying to eliminate entirely. Rep. Mark Takano (Calif.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms have struck parts of the South and Midwest, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off of homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Actor Val Kilmer dies at 65
item.description: Val Kilmer, a veteran actor known for his roles as Jim Morrison and Batman, has died on Tuesday, his family confirmed to the New York Times. He was 65. His daughter, Mercedes Kilmer, said her father died of pneumonia and was surrounded by family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, WGN 9

item.title: How to protect (and delete) your DNA data after 23andMe bankruptcy filing
item.description: The genetic testing company 23andMe recently filed for bankruptcy and announced it was looking to sell "substantially all of its assets." Here's what experts say that could mean for Utahns.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 6 people are injured after a truck crashed into a building and pedestrians in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A box truck crashed into a building Tuesday afternoon in Boston’s Chinatown neighborhood and hit several pedestrians before flipping onto its side, city officials said. The driver and five others were injured in what authorities said appears to have been an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities amid heated nationwide debates over the participation of trans youth in athletics.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: Man attempting to donate kidney detained by ICE
item.description: Immigration rights activists, community members and elected officials are calling on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to release a detainee who was preparing to make an organ donation to save his brother's life.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, KTLA 5

item.title: Cast for Beatles biopics revealed: Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, Joseph Quinn and Harris Dickinson to star
item.description: Sony Pictures kicked off the annual CinemaCon convention and trade show in Las Vegas on Monday by touting their four upcoming biopics told from the perspective of each member of The Beatles.
item.dc:creator: Krysta Fauria, Fox 5

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. About half of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, Fox 5

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
